forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,29 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KXJWHHWPYHXD78T104RVX7K6
+- 01KXJWKBK3F6QH05RQS0EFWS09
+- 01KXJWJCQ18DR0R8TQJKA3VK4W
+- 01KXJWJMT7M4ZND37N6RJ50YX5
+- 01KXJWKVHSPFD5TYG8B1CRX7KF
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8b80
+title: 'Tool fusion: FileTool.make(), op inference hook, aliases, read-only variant'
+---
+## What
+Per plan.md §3 vocabulary + §6 decisions 1–2. Create `Sources/FileTool/FileTool.swift`:
+- `FileTool.make(context:)` fusing the five `@Operation` structs into one `OperationTool` named `"files"` (description: "File operations for reading, writing, editing, and searching files.")
+- `FileTool.makeReadOnly(context:)` — fuses read/glob/grep only; `write file`/`edit file` → corrective "not available in read-only mode" (Rust `FileOperationSubset::ReadOnly` parity)
+- Missing-`op` inference hook (upstream's opt-in closure), Rust precedence: edit-ish keys (`edits`, find-ish, replace-ish) → `edit file`; `content` → `write file`; `pattern` + grep-marker key (`caseInsensitive`/`contextLines`/`outputMode`) → `grep files`; `pattern` alone → `glob files`; `path` alone → `read file`; undeterminable → corrective naming all five ops
+- Resolver alias declarations: `old_string`/`old`/`search`/`from`/`target`/`match` → `find`; `new_string`/`new`/`to`/`with`/`replacement` → `replace`; `absolute_path`/`file_path` ↔ `path`/`filePath` per op
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] All five ops dispatch through `AnyOperation` with typed outputs
+- [ ] A verbatim sah-style snake_case payload (`{"op":"edit file","file_path":…,"old_string":…,"new_string":…}`) lands as a working edit
+- [ ] Read-only tool rejects mutations with corrective messages and allows read/glob/grep
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/FileToolDispatchTests.swift`: dispatch per op; snake_case parity payloads; native-Edit-dialect (`old_string`/`new_string`) inference and aliasing; full inference matrix (every branch + undeterminable); read-only rejections + allowed ops; rendered schema contains exactly the five op strings
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter FileToolDispatchTests` — expect: green
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

-- Missing-`op` inference hook (upstream's opt-in closure), Rust precedence: edit-ish keys (`edits`, find-ish, replace-ish) → `edit file`; `content` → `write file`; `pattern` + grep-marker key (`caseInsensitive`/`contextLines`/`outputMode`) → `grep files`; `pattern` alone → `glob files`; `path` alone → `read file`; undeterminable → corrective naming all five ops
-- Resolver alias declarations: `old_string`/`old`/`search`/`from`/`target`/`match` → `find`; `new_string`/`new`/`to`/`with`/`replacement` → `replace`; `absolute_path`/`file_path` ↔ `path`/`filePath` per op
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] All five ops dispatch through `AnyOperation` with typed outputs
-- [ ] A verbatim sah-style snake_case payload (`{"op":"edit file","file_path":…,"old_string":…,"new_string":…}`) lands as a working edit
-- [ ] Read-only tool rejects mutations with corrective messages and allows read/glob/grep
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/FileToolDispatchTests.swift`: dispatch per op; snake_case parity payloads; native-Edit-dialect (`old_string`/`new_string`) inference and aliasing; full inference matrix (every branch + undeterminable); read-only rejections + allowed ops; rendered schema contains exactly the five op strings
-- [ ] Run `swift test --filter FileToolDispatchTests` — expect: green
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

   timestamp: 2026-07-16T23:48:00.248586+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kxpp1b09wwvda5v6emx69yh7
+  text: 'Research done. Design decided: (1) Parameter aliases MUST live on the @Operation structs as ParamMeta.aliases (the resolver only reads aliases from ParamMeta; OperationTool/AnyOperation expose no per-param alias injection). So adding @OperationParam(aliases:) to EditFile.find/replace/filePath, WriteFile.filePath, Read/Glob/Grep .path is the framework-idiomatic single source of alias data. No existing test asserts parameterMetadata, so this is safe. (2) FileTool.swift reuses OperationTool + OperationResolver(inferOp:) — no hand-rolled dispatcher. Inference is an ordered data table (InferenceRule[]) driving one code path; its key sets are DERIVED from the ops'' own ParamMeta (find/replace aliases, content, pattern, grep markers, path aliases) + the standalone `edits` marker, so alias literals are not duplicated between @OperationParam and the inference table. (3) makeReadOnly fuses read/glob/grep as real ops PLUS two empty @Generable rejector ops (verb write/edit file) that return WriteOutput/EditOutput.corrective("not available in read-only mode") — structural read-only (robust; does not rely on the caller setting a flag), and gives the specific corrective the task requires rather than unknownOperation. NOTE: EditFile.find/replace are [String]?, so the snake_case parity + native-Edit-dialect payloads pass old_string/new_string as arrays (EditFile''s array contract); the parity under test is the snake_case + native-Edit KEY aliasing, not scalar->array coercion.'
+  timestamp: 2026-07-17T00:01:19.881131+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXJWHHWPYHXD78T104RVX7K6
 - 01KXJWKBK3F6QH05RQS0EFWS09

+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kxppfxcp0z81tcwh9eh1qkt9
+  text: 'Implemented + GREEN. Sources/FileTool/FileTool.swift created: FileTool.make(context:) fuses ReadFile/WriteFile/EditFile/GlobFiles/GrepFiles into one OperationTool \"files\" (desc \"File operations for reading, writing, editing, and searching files.\"); makeReadOnly(context:) fuses read/glob/grep real + two @Generable @Operation rejector stubs (ReadOnlyWriteFile/ReadOnlyEditFile) returning WriteOutput/EditOutput.corrective(\"The `write file`/`edit file` operation is not available in read-only mode.\") — structural read-only, never touches disk. Reuses OperationTool + OperationResolver(inferOp:) — no hand-rolled dispatcher. Inference is a data-driven ordered InferenceRule table (one matches() predicate) with key sets DERIVED from the ops'' own ParamMeta aliases + the standalone `edits` marker (no alias literal duplicated). Aliases declared via @OperationParam on the 5 op structs (find/replace/path per op). FOUND+FIXED a real bug: upstream OperationResolver.defaultVerbAliases maps read->get, which rewrote our `read file` op and made it unmatchable (returned unknownOperation); added fileVerbSelfAliases (each file verb -> itself, derived from op .verb statics) to neutralize it. Tests/FileToolTests/FileToolDispatchTests.swift: 29 tests — dispatch per op, snake_case parity edit (file_path/old_string/new_string, arrays per EditFile''s [String] contract) lands on disk, native-Edit-dialect inference+aliasing, full inference matrix incl precedence + undeterminable, read-only rejections + allowed ops, schema op-enum == exactly five for both variants. `swift test --filter FileToolDispatchTests`: 29 passed. Full `swift test`: 240 unit + 1 integration scaffold passed, zero warnings/errors from Sources/Tests. Awaiting adversarial double-check; leaving task in doing.'

Sources/FileTool/FileTool.swift created: FileTool.make(context:) fuses ReadFile/WriteFile/EditFile/GlobFiles/GrepFiles into one OperationTool \"files\" (desc \"File operations for reading, writing, editing, and searching files.\"); makeReadOnly(context:) fuses read/glob/grep real + two @Generable @Operation rejector stubs (ReadOnlyWriteFile/ReadOnlyEditFile) returning WriteOutput/EditOutput.corrective(\"The `write file`/`edit file` operation is not available in read-only mode.\") — structural read-only, never touches disk. Reuses OperationTool + OperationResolver(inferOp:) — no hand-rolled dispatcher. Inference is a data-driven ordered InferenceRule table (one matches() predicate) with key sets DERIVED from the ops'' own ParamMeta aliases + the standalone `edits` marker (no alias literal duplicated). Aliases declared via @OperationParam on the 5 op structs (find/replace/path per op). FOUND+FIXED a real bug: upstream OperationResolver.defaultVerbAliases maps read->get, which rewrote our `read file` op and made it unmatchable (returned unknownOperation); added fileVerbSelfAliases (each file verb -> itself, derived from op .verb statics) to neutralize it. Tests/FileToolTests/FileToolDispatchTests.swift: 29 tests — dispatch per op, snake_case parity edit (file_path/old_string/new_string, arrays per EditFile''s [String] contract) lands on disk, native-Edit-dialect inference+aliasing, full inference matrix incl precedence + undeterminable, read-only rejections + allowed ops, schema op-enum == exactly five for both variants. `swift test --filter FileToolDispatchTests`: 29 passed.
